title: Dasymaschalon filipes
intro: Dasymaschalon filipes, commonly known as the forked fruit or forked fruit tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dasymaschalon filipes include alkaloids, particularly dasymaschaline and dasymaschalidine, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. In traditional medicine, Dasymaschalon filipes is often processed into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever and rheumatism.
